channel.title: Captain Dadders — Flight Expeditions
channel.description: Following the Lewis & Clark Corps of Discovery route by air — MSFS 2024 expedition blog.

item.title: Leg 5: Queen City to the Fossil Lick — KLUK to 4KT4
item.description: Departing Cincinnati's Lunken Field past the Great American Ballpark and the Bengals' stadium, then a short hop downriver to Steele's Bottom — a grass strip chosen for its proximity to Big Bone Lick, where Lewis made his famous mastodon fossil detour in October 1803.

item.title: Leg 4: River Highway to Cincinnati — I41 to KLUK
item.description: Crossing out of West Virginia with Kentucky on the left and Ohio on the right, following the old westward migration corridor past barges and towboats, climbing through morning fog, and finally picking up the Cincinnati skyline — with a first paved landing of the trip at Lunken Field.

item.title: Leg 3: Down to Huntington — 75D to I41
item.description: The longest leg yet — climbing above river fog just after takeoff, descending over farmland and a parade of power plants, and reaching Robert Newlon Field near Huntington WV with not much fuel to spare and a ground loop narrowly avoided on landing.

item.title: Leg 2: Into West Virginia — 1G8 to 75D
item.description: Continuing down the Ohio past Wheeling and its distinctive river island, over the ancient Grave Creek Mound at Moundsville that Lewis himself climbed in 1803, and landing at the former grass strip at Steelton, New Martinsville WV — now a park, but still usable in MSFS 2024.

item.title: Leg 1: Departing Pittsburgh — 31D to 1G8
item.description: The expedition gets underway. Following the Ohio River from Inter County Airport past The Point in Pittsburgh to Eddie Dew Memorial Airpark in Toronto, Ohio — the same ground Lewis and Clark covered August 31 to September 5, 1803.
